codeReview.summary: Review of commit 352c3689edffa33856f47a66796545341f48b7dc found the feature contract covered by focused regression additions only. The committed tests now exercise direct runtime-resolver auto-init, Store CRUD auto-ensure, default-vs-override isolation, and later first-use default mutation without revealing any contract gaps in the implementation under review.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker repeatedly relied on the GitNexus CLI to satisfy repo policy, but .factory/services.yaml documents only cargo commands and no GitNexus command surface or availability expectations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification commands include npx gitnexus impact resolve_crypto_context_for --repo appdb --direction upstream and failed npx gitnexus detect_changes --repo appdb --scope all; .factory/services.yaml lists only install/test/lint/build/typecheck cargo commands.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill tells workers to fall back when GitNexus tooling is unavailable, but it does not state the concrete CLI limitations observed here or the exact fallback sequence to use.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The feature handoff skillFeedback suggests documenting the exact GitNexus CLI fallback command set; .factory/skills/rust-library-worker/SKILL.md only says to note the fallback and use direct source inspection plus grep/diff review.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, commit diff, transcript skeleton, mission contract, and relevant shared-state files. The commit is a test-only proof pass: it adds authoritative regressions for auto-registration runtime behavior and I found no blocking or non-blocking code issues in the reviewed implementation.
